name: Saudi Arabia
summary: Day 77 of Hormuz blockade. **A8 SAUDI MFA TACIT ACKNOWLEDGMENT LANGUAGE ISSUED — TEHRAN INSTITUTIONAL SILENCE HOLDS** (REPORTED — Reuters primary; Saudi MFA statement direct primary but does not address strikes). A senior Saudi foreign ministry official, asked directly whether covert strikes had been carried out, did not address the question, and instead said: 'We reaffirm Saudi Arabia's consistent position advocating de-escalation, self-restraint and the reduction of tensions in pursuit of the stability, security and prosperity.' **The careful construction is the closest thing to a tacit acknowledgment in any Saudi communication of the cycle — the official did NOT deny the Reuters/WSJ reporting.** The Iranian foreign ministry 'did not respond to a request for comment' per Reuters. **Reuters also added the timing detail: 'informal de-escalation took effect in the week before Washington and Tehran agreed to a ceasefire in their broader conflict on April 7'** — Saudi-Iran de-escalation predated the broader US-Iran ceasefire by approximately a week. **This is operationally important: it suggests Saudi exit from the kinetic exchange may have been the leading edge of the broader ceasefire architecture, a structural insight the graph should now encode.** Al Hadath, Arab News and Al Arabiya remain editorially silent on both the WSJ Lavan and the Reuters Saudi-strikes pieces — five-day Gulf-press embarrassment-management pattern. Tadawul (TASI) 10,995.44 (-0.2% 1D, -5.1% 1M, -2.1% 3M) = Saudi NOT participating in EM rally — frontline-state-exposure premium being un-priced; cumulative -2.1% 3M reflects gradual Saudi-fiscal-runway pricing-in. **B3 MODI ARRIVES UAE MAY 15 — first leg of 5-nation tour; UAE Minister Al Jaber publicly framed Hormuz situation as 'weaponised'**; Saudi visibly distancing from UAE military track. Iran has not publicly responded to either WSJ or Reuters disclosures. EIA May STEO carries forward: Gulf states collectively shut in 10.5 MMbpd in April; Saudi-Emirati supply ~90% preserved via East-West reroute; Aramco CEO Nasser 2-5 ships/day Hormuz unchanged. Bloomberg May 13 satellite Kharg jetties empty again. World Bank $86 + Goldman $85 + JPM $60 = institutional band still threatens Saudi fiscal runway (~$95/bbl break-even); Sadara JV ($3.7B debt, June 15 grace expiry) trigger UNCHANGED. **A10 REUTERS NOW AMPLIFIES THE SAUDI-STRIKES STORY (was Newsweek/WSJ-only cluster yesterday) — Reuters May 12-13 cited 'two Western officials and two Iranian officials'** (CONFIRMED — Reuters primary; carried by US News, Spokesman, Times of Israel, Haaretz 'Saudi Arabia Covertly Struck Iran During War,' Kathmandu Post, Pravda, BusinessWorld Online). **MATERIALLY STRENGTHENS BASIS** from one-cluster (Newsweek-WSJ) to multi-cluster (WSJ + Reuters as independent primary scoops). Reuters adds substantive new intelligence: **'Saudi Arabia made Iran aware of the strikes and this was followed by intensive diplomatic engagement and Saudi threats to retaliate further, which led to an understanding between the two countries to de-escalate.'** The Riyadh-Tehran channel therefore reads as 'kinetic strikes followed by de-escalation understanding' — STRUCTURALLY DIFFERENT from the UAE-Iran reading (UAE remains a fully active third combatant per WSJ; UAE foreign ministry has not retreated). **SAUDI-IRAN TRAJECTORY NOW BIFURCATING FROM UAE-IRAN TRAJECTORY: Riyadh has paid the price of engagement and exited; Abu Dhabi remains operationally engaged.** Editor must still respect tag floor — Saudi-strikes remain REPORTED, not CONFIRMED, because neither Riyadh nor Tehran has institutionally acknowledged. But the basis has moved from one-cluster to multi-cluster, which justifies an upward weight adjustment in the graph from 9.0 toward 9.5. Saudi MoFA still silent; Iranian MoFA still silent (per Reuters explicit non-comment); Al Hadath / Arab News / Al Arabiya editorial silence continues (no piece confronting either WSJ or Reuters disclosure). Israel side: JNS picked up the story; Times of Israel covered; Haaretz covered. Iran International (opposition, Saudi-funded) covered with implicit approval framing. Tadawul (TASI) 11,020.07 (-0.2% 1D, -4.1% 1M) = Saudi NOT participating in MSCI EM +2.1% rally — direct frontline-state-exposure premium being progressively un-priced. **EIA May STEO published May 12 (CONFIRMED): Iraq + Saudi Arabia + Kuwait + UAE + Qatar + Bahrain collectively shut in 10.5 MMbpd in April; Saudi-Emirati supply preserved ~90% via East-West reroute (carries forward); Aramco CEO Nasser May 11 line '2-5 ships/day, market won't normalise until 2027 if Hormuz persists past mid-June' carries forward unrefuted; Bloomberg May 13 satellite shows Kharg jetties empty again.** PRIOR (May 13): On top of yesterday's UAE-Lavan WSJ revelation. Saudi has not publicly acknowledged the late-March strikes; Iran has not commented on the Saudi disclosure; Al Hadath (Saudi-state) ran editorial distance language on UAE story but has not engaged the Saudi-strikes claim — same kid-gloves silence as on Lavan. Arab News + Al Arabiya (Saudi state-aligned) handled Saudi-strikes disclosure with editorial silence (neither denying nor confirming) — DELIBERATE positioning consistent with MBS's operational-deniability + public-pro-mediation playbook.** **The Gulf-alignment graph now requires Saudi to move from 'OPEC+/balancing/underwriter' toward partial kinetic participant — STRUCTURAL RE-ENCODING OF ENTIRE GULF ROW.** Saudi FM Faisal bin Farhan's trilateral calls to Araghchi + Dar (Pakistan) continue per yesterday — Riyadh maintaining 'underwriter not party' public posture even as US reporting paints them as covert party. Modi arrives in UAE May 15 (UAE confirmed covert combatant); Modi's Gulf-cousin visit lands in a more politically loaded environment than even yesterday's reading anticipated; Modi-Iran-FM-meet in Delhi May 14 brackets the trip. **B3 ARAMCO STRATEGIC NARRATIVE CONFIRMED: Aramco CEO Nasser May 11 line ('2-5 ships/day, market won't normalise until 2027 if Hormuz persists past mid-June') carries forward. Newly surfaced corollaries: Q1 2026 PROFIT +25% YoY off East-West pipeline reroute (Yanbu exports 770K bpd Jan-Feb → ~4 MMbpd late March → 3.66-4 MMbpd through mid-May; East-West pipeline capacity pushed to 7 MMbpd); Aramco + ADNOC reportedly 'quietly resumed limited Hormuz crude exports' per World Oil May 8. Saudi-Emirati supply has been ~90% preserved through non-Hormuz routing + quiet limited Hormuz transits, even as headline non-Iranian Hormuz throughput shows -96% (EIA: avg ~500,000 bpd non-Iranian crude since early March vs ~13.6 MMbpd pre-war). This is why Brent has not gone parabolic despite operational reality — Gulf-state pipeline optionality is the saving grace.** Tadawul (TASI) 11,039.23 (-1.1% 1D, -3.4% 1M, -1.9% 3M) — modest decline on WSJ Saudi-strikes disclosure, less than expected because (a) slow-amplification curve (Newsweek primary, US wires lagging), (b) Saudi state-aligned outlets editorial silence, (c) Aramco Q1 +25% via East-West rerouting is strong offsetting fundamental tailwind, (d) market treating Saudi covert combatant status as already-known information (disclosure date ≠ participation event). World Bank $86 + Goldman $85 + JPM $60 = institutional band still threatens Saudi fiscal runway (~$95/bbl break-even) but Goldman revision is bullish-marginal. Sadara JV ($3.7B debt, June 15 grace expiry) trigger UNCHANGED. ORIGINAL CONTEXT (May 12): **A12 SAUDI FM PRINCE FAISAL BIN FARHAN CALLED IRANIAN FM ARAGHCHI AND PAKISTANI FM ISHAQ DAR SEPARATELY MAY 11 TO DISCUSS 'PAKISTAN'S MEDIATION EFFORTS.'** Pakistan formally still official mediator; Qatar substantive (consistent with May 10 Axios scoop on Rubio/Witkoff/al-Thani Miami meeting). The multi-channel architecture (Pakistan official + Qatar substantive + Saudi underwriter + Russia uranium-custodian standing offer) is now publicly visible. Pakistan source: 'no immediate danger' of war resumption; back-channels active despite public harshness. **B6 WSJ MAY 11 REVELATION REFRAMES SAUDI POSITIONING: UAE secretly struck Iran's Lavan refinery April 8 — Saudi visibly distancing from UAE's military track. Saudi public posture (operational deniability + public-pro-mediation) is now READING AS DELIBERATE DISTINCTION from UAE's only-third-combatant status.** The Saudi public denial via Al-Hadath May 8 (no offensive-airspace use) is more meaningful in light of WSJ Lavan revelation — it's not just a public-relations posture, it's an explicit positioning AGAINST UAE's military track. Qatari and Saudi MoFAs remained publicly silent on the Doha bulk carrier strike (Day 2 of 7-day trigger window). The 'Iran responds via Qatar-IRGC channel rather than Pakistan-FM channel within 7 days' trigger (added May 10) is at Day 2 of 7 — Iran's formal response on May 10-11 went via PAKISTAN per public reporting (no Qatar-IRGC channel attribution); substantive-mediator-via-Qatar trigger NOT yet confirmed by operational signal. **Aramco CEO Nasser May 11: Hormuz transit 2-5 ships/day (DETERIORATED from May 10 ~6/day), 100M bbl/week lost, 880M cumulative, market won't normalize until 2027 if disruption persists past mid-June** — industry-CEO authority. Aramco runs the only adjacent-scale crude operation; Nasser is unlikely to overstate disruption which hurts his customer base. Tadawul (TASI) 11,158.45 (+0.4% 1D, -1.4% 1M, -0.1% 3M, essentially flat vs pre-crisis 11,170). World Bank $86 average for 2026 + Goldman raised 2026 average to $85 (from $77) + JPM $60 = institutional band still threatens Saudi fiscal runway (~$95/bbl break-even) but Goldman revision is bullish-marginal. Sadara JV ($3.7B debt, June 15 grace expiry) trigger UNCHANGED. — Bahrain detained 41 alleged IRGC-linked individuals May 9 (first major Gulf-state internal action against alleged IRGC network); Qatar formally re-emerged as parallel-track substantive mediator via IRGC senior generals (Axios scoop, Rubio/Witkoff/al-Thani Miami meeting Saturday); UAE air defense engagement May 8 confirmed 3 wounded.** Saudi public posture continues: operational deniability + public alignment with Pakistan-Iran de-escalation track. Saudi May 9 statement endorsing Pakistan's mediation (Radio Pakistan, Arab News) reinforces public-facing posture. **No Saudi MoFA statement on the Sunday bulk-carrier-near-Doha strike at filing time — despite Qatar being a fellow GCC member in same coastal waters.** The Gulf-state response to Iran is now openly bifurcated: Bahrain (pro-US, anti-IRGC), Saudi Arabia (deniable-pro-US-operationally, public-pro-mediation), UAE (continues hawkish operationally), Qatar (now openly mediating with IRGC contacts), Oman (silent mediator), Kuwait (denied airspace per WSJ scoop). PRIOR FRAMING (preserved): Saudi Al-Hadath May 8 denial of WSJ offensive-airspace-lift scoop = MBS playbook (operational deniability + public alignment with Iranian-negotiating-side mediation track). Tadawul +0.7% May 8 close (Friday partial trading day; Sunday open will be first reaction to Doha strike + Qatar mediator reframing). World Bank $86 average for 2026 + Goldman Q2 $90 = institutional forecast band threatens Saudi fiscal runway (~$95/bbl break-even). Sadara JV ($3.7B debt, June 15 grace expiry) trigger UNCHANGED. Following Wednesday's WSJ scoop that Saudi Arabia and Kuwait had denied US base/airspace access for Project Freedom and that those restrictions had since been 'lifted,' Saudi officials briefed Washington's press on background while 'the Saudi government denies the story on the record' (CONFIRMED — The DuPree Report, Türkiye Today). An unnamed Saudi source told Saudi state-aligned broadcaster Al-Hadath: 'the Kingdom did not allow the use of its airspace to support offensive military operations' and Riyadh was 'supporting de-escalation and Pakistan's efforts to broker a peace agreement' (CONFIRMED — Türkiye Today citing Al-Hadath primary). Iran International and Times of Israel separately note Saudi/Kuwait have lifted restrictions per US officials, but Saudi official line is now public denial on the record. **Structural read:** Saudi is doing two things simultaneously — (a) operationally permitting whatever US needs (per on-background US-officials lift narrative), (b) publicly DENYING any offensive support and aligning rhetorically with Pakistan-Iran de-escalation. This is the cleanest single example of MBS's playbook in cycle — operational deniability paired with public alignment with Iranian-negotiating-side mediation track. The 'Saudi formally named primary mediator (or co-mediator) for US-Iran Hormuz channel within 14 days (by 2026-05-12)' trigger from qatar.json — if reframed for Saudi — is now within reach: Saudi has publicly endorsed Pakistan's mediation, which is itself a soft-mediator posture. Tadawul +0.7% May 8 (treated cautiously — Friday partial trading day; Sunday open will be first reaction to Al-Hadath denial). PRIOR FRAMING (preserved): Saudi MBS extended silence on UAE OPEC exit; Tadawul -0.5% peace-dividend-bearish signal; Aramco fell 4.7% after-hours on UAE exit; UAE departure means ADNOC can undercut Saudi pricing. World Bank $86 average for 2026 + Goldman Q2 $90 = institutional forecast band threatens Saudi fiscal runway (~$95/bbl break-even). Chatham House (May 2026): MBS posture 'strategic recalibration.' Sadara JV ($3.7B debt, June 15 grace expiry) trigger UNCHANGED.

active_triggers.condition: Yanbu pipeline capacity maxed out or sustained attack closes it
Sadara JV ($3.7B debt) defaults at June 15 grace expiry without operational recovery
Saudi formally named primary mediator (or co-mediator) for US-Iran Hormuz channel within 14 days (by 2026-05-22)
active_triggers.note: Day 1 of 14 as of 2026-05-09. Saudi public denial via Al-Hadath + endorsement of Pakistan mediation = soft proxy already; formal naming within 14 days would be the firmer signal.

recent_signals.headline: **A10 REUTERS NOW AMPLIFIES THE SAUDI-STRIKES STORY** — Reuters May 12-13 cited 'two Western officials and two Iranian officials' (CONFIRMED — Reuters primary; carried by US News, Spokesman, Times of Israel, Haaretz, Kathmandu Post, Pravda, BusinessWorld Online). Materially STRENGTHENS basis from one-cluster (Newsweek-WSJ) to multi-cluster (WSJ + Reuters independent primary scoops). **Reuters adds new substantive intelligence: 'Saudi Arabia made Iran aware of the strikes and this was followed by intensive diplomatic engagement and Saudi threats to retaliate further, which led to an understanding between the two countries to de-escalate.'** Riyadh-Tehran channel now reads as 'kinetic strikes followed by de-escalation understanding' — STRUCTURALLY DIFFERENT from UAE-Iran reading (UAE remains active third combatant; UAE foreign ministry has not retreated). **SAUDI-IRAN/UAE-IRAN TRAJECTORIES BIFURCATING.** Saudi MoFA still silent; Iranian MoFA still silent. Al Hadath / Arab News / Al Arabiya editorial silence continues (no piece confronting either WSJ or Reuters disclosure). Iran International (opposition, Saudi-funded) covered with implicit approval framing. **Editor tag floor: Saudi-strikes remain REPORTED, not CONFIRMED — neither Riyadh nor Tehran has institutionally acknowledged. Basis upgraded from one-cluster to multi-cluster justifies graph weight bump 9.0 → 9.5.** TASI 11,020.07 (-0.2% 1D, -4.1% 1M) = Saudi NOT participating in MSCI EM +2.1% rally; direct frontline-state-exposure premium being progressively un-priced. **EIA May STEO May 12 (CONFIRMED): Iraq + Saudi + Kuwait + UAE + Qatar + Bahrain collectively shut in 10.5 MMbpd in April; Brent forecast $89 4Q26.** Saudi-Emirati supply preserved ~90% via East-West reroute carries forward; Aramco CEO Nasser 2-5 ships/day Hormuz carries forward.
sources: Reuters 'Exclusive-Saudi Arabia Launched Covert Attacks on Iran as Regional War Widened — Sources' May 12-13 primary
Times of Israel 'Saudi Arabia covertly launched strikes on Iran during war, sources say'
Haaretz 'Saudi Arabia Covertly Struck Iran During War, Sources Say'
JNS 'UAE, Saudi Arabia conducted secret strikes against Iran'
RT (Russian state) + US News + Kathmandu Post + BusinessWorld Online (wires)
Iran International (opposition, Saudi-funded, with implicit approval framing)
EIA May STEO May 12 (Gulf shut-in 10.5 MMbpd April)
Bloomberg 'Hormuz Oil Shipments Drop Nearly 30% in First Quarter, EIA Reports' May 13
recent_signals.verification: Reuters Saudi-strikes amplification + de-escalation understanding = REPORTED (multi-cluster basis strengthened — WSJ + Reuters independent primary scoops; Saudi/Iran institutionally silent — NOT promoted to CONFIRMED); EIA Gulf shut-in 10.5 MMbpd = CONFIRMED (EIA primary); TASI tape = CONFIRMED (yfinance).

recent_signals.headline: A10 MAJOR DISCLOSURE COMPOUNDED: Newsweek + WSJ + 2 Western officials + 2 Iranian officials reported May 12-13 that SAUDI ARABIA LAUNCHED SECRET STRIKES AGAINST IRAN IN LATE MARCH ('tit-for-tat retaliation when Saudi was hit') — REPORTED (WSJ primary, single-source cluster). On top of yesterday's UAE-Lavan WSJ revelation. Saudi has NOT publicly acknowledged; Iran silent on Saudi disclosure; Al Hadath (Saudi state) ran editorial distance on UAE story but has NOT engaged Saudi-strikes claim — same kid-gloves silence as on Lavan. Arab News + Al Arabiya (Saudi state-aligned) editorial silence (neither denying nor confirming). The Gulf-alignment graph now requires Saudi to move from 'OPEC+/balancing/underwriter' toward partial kinetic participant = STRUCTURAL RE-ENCODING. Saudi FM Faisal bin Farhan's trilateral calls to Araghchi + Pakistan FM Dar continue per yesterday — Riyadh maintaining 'underwriter not party' public posture even as US reporting paints them as covert party. **B3 ARAMCO STRATEGIC NARRATIVE CONFIRMED: Q1 2026 PROFIT +25% YoY off East-West pipeline reroute (Yanbu exports 770K bpd Jan-Feb → ~4 MMbpd late March → 3.66-4 MMbpd through mid-May; East-West pipeline pushed to 7 MMbpd capacity); Aramco + ADNOC reportedly 'quietly resumed limited Hormuz crude exports' (World Oil May 8). Saudi-Emirati supply has been ~90% preserved through non-Hormuz routing + quiet limited Hormuz transits despite headline non-Iranian Hormuz throughput -96%. Explains why Brent has not gone parabolic — Gulf-state pipeline optionality saving grace. Without it, Goldman-vs-JPM $25 gap would have resolved Goldman direction already.** Tadawul (TASI) 11,039.23 (-1.1% 1D, -3.4% 1M) — modest decline on WSJ Saudi-strikes disclosure, less than expected because: (a) slow-amplification curve (Newsweek primary, US wires lagging); (b) Saudi state-aligned outlets editorial silence; (c) Aramco Q1 +25% via East-West rerouting is strong offsetting fundamental tailwind; (d) market treating Saudi covert combatant status as already-known information. World Bank $86 + Goldman $85 + JPM $60 = institutional band still threatens Saudi fiscal runway (~$95/bbl break-even). Sadara JV ($3.7B debt, June 15 grace expiry) trigger UNCHANGED.
sources: Newsweek 'Iran War Update: Saudi Arabia and UAE Involved Militarily'
WSJ primary (cited by Newsweek + ToI + Middle East Eye)
Al-Hadath (Saudi state, editorial distance on UAE)
Arab News + Al Arabiya (Saudi state-aligned, editorial silence)
World Oil 'Aramco ADNOC quietly resume limited Hormuz crude exports'
recent_signals.verification: Saudi strikes Iran late March = REPORTED (Newsweek+WSJ single primary cluster; Saudi silent; Iran silent; Saudi state-aligned editorial silence on disclosure) — DO NOT ELEVATE; Aramco Q1 +25% = CONFIRMED (corporate financials, multi-source); ADNOC+Aramco quiet Hormuz resume = REPORTED (World Oil industry primary); Tadawul -1.1% = CONFIRMED yfinance tape.

recent_signals.headline: GULF-STATE BIFURCATION DEEPENS — BAHRAIN DETAINED 41 ALLEGED IRGC-LINKED MAY 9 (first major internal Gulf action vs IRGC network); QATAR FORMALLY RE-EMERGED AS PARALLEL-TRACK MEDIATOR VIA IRGC SENIOR GENERALS (Axios scoop, Rubio/Witkoff/al-Thani Miami Saturday); UAE AIR-DEFENSE ENGAGEMENT MAY 8 CONFIRMED 3 WOUNDED (UPDATED — yesterday underplayed scale: 2 ballistic + 3 drones); NO SAUDI MoFA STATEMENT ON SUNDAY BULK-CARRIER-NEAR-DOHA STRIKE DESPITE GCC FELLOW-MEMBER STATUS. Saudi May 9 endorsement of Pakistan's mediation via Radio Pakistan / Arab News reinforces public-facing posture. The Gulf bifurcation is now operationally specific: Bahrain (pro-US, anti-IRGC), Saudi (deniable-pro-US-operationally + public-pro-mediation), UAE (hawkish operationally), Qatar (mediating with IRGC contacts), Oman (silent), Kuwait (denied airspace). MBS playbook continues: operational deniability + public alignment with Iranian-negotiating-side mediation track.
sources: Khaleej Times (Bahrain MoI primary, Saudi-region pickup)
Times of Israel May 9-10 live blogs
Radio Pakistan (Saudi endorsement of Pakistan mediation May 9)
recent_signals.verification: Bahrain detentions = CONFIRMED ACTION (Bahrain MoI primary, multi-source); Qatar mediator emergence = REPORTED (Axios primary scoop, multi-corroborated); UAE air defense engagement = CONFIRMED multi-source (UAE Defense Ministry primary, 3 wounded confirmed); Saudi MoFA = CONFIRMED ABSENCE of statement on Doha strike at filing time

recent_signals.headline: SAUDI PUBLICLY DENIED WSJ SCOOP ON OFFENSIVE-AIRSPACE LIFT — OFFICIAL POSITION IS THAT AIRSPACE WAS NEVER OPENED FOR US OFFENSIVE OPERATIONS DURING PROJECT FREEDOM. Saudi officials briefed Washington's press on background while 'the Saudi government denies the story on the record.' Unnamed Saudi source via Al-Hadath: 'the Kingdom did not allow the use of its airspace to support offensive military operations,' Riyadh 'supporting de-escalation and Pakistan's efforts to broker a peace agreement.' Iran International + Times of Israel note Saudi/Kuwait have lifted restrictions per US officials, but Saudi official line is now public denial on the record. MBS playbook: operational deniability + public alignment with Iranian-negotiating-side mediation track. The 'Saudi formally named primary mediator (or co-mediator) within 14 days (by 2026-05-12)' watchpoint is now within reach: Saudi has publicly endorsed Pakistan mediation = soft-mediator posture. Tadawul +0.7% May 8 (Friday partial trading day; Sunday open will be first reaction to Al-Hadath denial).
recent_signals.verification: Saudi public denial via Al-Hadath = CONFIRMED DIPLOMATIC SIGNAL on the record (Türkiye Today citing Al-Hadath primary); WSJ underlying scoop = CONFIRMED REPORTED via wire; US-officials lift narrative = REPORTED via background; Tadawul price = CONFIRMED yfinance

recent_signals.headline: TADAWUL -0.5% ONLY MAJOR EQUITY INDEX IN RED ON GLOBAL RISK-ON DAY — peace dividend BEARISH for war's primary fiscal beneficiary. Aramco fell 4.7% after-hours on UAE OPEC exit news; compounds Brent $107→$101 round-trip. ADNOC can undercut Saudi pricing (Murban discount potentially widening to $3.50/bbl by Q3 — WoodMac). Saudi break-even ~$95/bbl; World Bank $86 average for 2026 + Goldman Q2 $90 threaten fiscal runway. MBS Day 9 of 14 silence on UAE OPEC exit AND May 4 Iran-Fujairah strike. Chatham House: 'strategic recalibration' framing. UAE foreign minister rather than ruler at Jeddah summit — deepening diplomatic distance.
sources: Axios 'Year that shook the Gulf' May 2
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED Tadawul -0.5% (yfinance multi-source); CONFIRMED Aramco after-hours -4.7% on UAE OPEC exit; CONFIRMED MBS silence pattern via institutional analysis (Chatham House, Atlantic Council); REPORTED ADNOC Murban discount projection (WoodMac base case)

historical_summaries.summary: 6 signals. Saudi missiles intercepted | Saudi revived ~50% of oil exports via Yanbu Red Sea bypass | Iran attacked Yanbu port with drone, briefly halting loadings

24 signals. WaPo national security desk: Gulf allies privately urging Trump to continue fighting 'until Iran is decisively defeated | Saudi Arabia expelled Iranian military staff (persona non grata), citing Article 51 self-defense and UNSC Resolution 2817 | Saudi expelled Iranian military staff — persona non grata, citing Article 51 self-defense — effectively reversing the 2023 Beijing Agreement
